data.title: Jefferson Capital files for IPO
data.description:
Charged-off and insolvency consumer accounts manager Jefferson Capital has filed a registration statement with the SEC for its proposed IPO.

The company intends to list its shares of common stock on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol JCAP.

The number of shares to be offered in the IPO and the price range are yet to be determined.

Form S-1

data.title: Snowflake jumps as Wall Street praises sales changes, new products
data.description:
Snowflake's (NYSE:SNOW) strong first-quarter and increased guidance are evidence that its new products and the changes the company made to its sales operation are resonating with customers, Wall Street analysts said.

Shares rose 10% in premarket trading on the back of the quarterly results and raised guidance.

Morgan Stanley analyst Keith Weiss — who reiterated his Equal-Weight rating on Snowflake due to valuation but raised his price target to $200 from $185 — said the February 2024 appointment of CEO Sridhar Ramaswamy has turned Snowflake around. The company has accelerated the pace of product innovation, driven better alignment with sales and go-to-market teams, and improved its ability to address artificial intelligence workloads and unstructured data. 

“Q1 was a clean and impressive quarter, and we come away more convicted Snowflake's secular growth prospects have improved in the eyes of investors versus this time last year,” Weiss wrote in a note to clients. “A stable core, coupled with building momentum in data engineering and early signs of traction in AI and applications, sets the stage for durable 20%+ growth over the next several years with operating margins heading higher.” 

Jefferies analyst Brent Thill said the results were an “all-around clean print” and evidence that new product momentum is picking up, specifically citing Snowpark and Dynamic Tables. 

“We reiterate SNOW as our top recovery story,” Thill, who maintained his Buy rating and $220 price target, wrote in a note to clients.

Needham analyst Mike Cikos raised his price target on Snowflake to $230 from $215 and reiterated his Buy rating after the results.  

“We view accelerated Headcount growth for both COGS and Sales & Marketing in 1QFY26 as indicative of Snowflake seeding and reaccelerating future Product Revenue growth, and believe the company sustains outsized beats versus the updated FY26 Product Revenue guide,” Cikos wrote.

data.title: Delcath sets outlook amid plans to enter National Medicaid Drug Rebate Agreement
data.description:
Delcath Systems (NASDAQ:DCTH) traded higher in the premarket on Thursday after the MedTech issued its full-year revenue outlook and disclosed plans to widen market access by entering into a Medicaid National Drug Rebate Agreement (NDRA).

The New York-based firm said it has started discussions with the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services to enter an NDRA, and the agreement is expected to take effect in Q3 2025.  

NDRA will enable the company to take part in the Medicaid Drug Rebate Program, which allows qualifying healthcare establishments to receive Medicaid rebates and 340B discounts.

“Our decision to enter into the NDRA simplifies Medicaid access and enables eligible hospitals to access 340B drug pricing,” CEO Gerard Michel remarked, adding that it will accelerate the market availability of the company’s drug-device combination, Hepzato.

Looking ahead, the company said it expects Hepzato treatment volume to rise at least 200% YoY in 2025 and revenue from Hepzato Kit and its drug delivery system Chemosat to reach $94M-$98M, implying over 150% YoY growth. The consensus estimates suggest $94.5M in revenue for Delcath (NASDAQ:DCTH) in 2025.

data.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman, former Apple exec Jony Ive talk about new AI device with staff - report
data.description:
OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman gave his staff a preview of the devices he is developing to build with former Apple designer Jony Ive, paving plans to ship 100 million AI “companions” that he hopes will become a part of life, The Wall Street Journal reported.

The news comes on the heels of Sir Jony Ive, the former Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) executive best known for his design work on the iPhone and Mac, joining OpenAI in a $6.5B deal, which was announced on Wednesday. Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)-backed OpenAI is paying $5B in stock to acquire Ives' hardware startup, io. The remaining $1.5B comes from a 23% stake that OpenAI acquired in io in the fourth quarter of 2024.

Altman told staff that they had “the chance to do the biggest thing we’ve ever done as a company here,” the report added.

OpenAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Ive and his design consultancy firm LoveFrom will assume deep design and creative responsibilities across OpenAI and io, OpenAI had said on Wednesday.

The acquisition has the potential to add $1T in value to OpenAI, the report added.

Altman and Ive provided a few hints at the secret project they have been working on. The product will be capable of being fully aware of a user’s surroundings and life. It will be unobtrusive, able to be placed in one’s pocket or on one’s desk, and would be a third core device a person would put on their desk after a MacBook Pro and an iPhone, according to the report.

The media outlet reported earlier that the product would not be a phone, and that Ive and Altman’s aim is to help users let go of screens. Altman said the device also is not a pair of glasses, and that Ive had been skeptical about building something to wear on the body, the report noted.

Ive referenced “a new design movement.” Altman noted that it would amount to a “family of devices,” showing his liking for how Apple has long integrated its hardware and software offerings.

Altman told OpenAI employees that stealth will be vital for their success to avoid rivals copying the product before it is ready.

For months, Ive’s team has been talking to vendors who will be able to ship the device at scale.

“We’re not going to ship 100 million devices literally on day one,” said Altman, forecasting that OpenAI would ship that large number of high-quality devices “faster than any company has ever shipped 100 million of something new before.” Altman said the aim is to release a device by late next year.

Ive and Altman also provided details about how their collaboration grew in the last several years. About 18 months ago, OpenAI’s Vice President of Product Peter Welinder started working with Ive’s team. The two parties became excited about a specific device last fall, the report noted.

The original plan was that Ive’s startup would develop and sell its own devices using OpenAI’s technology. However, Altman said he realized that would not work and that knew the two companies would have to be combined because the device was not only an accessory but a central facet of the user relationship with OpenAI, the report stated.

Apple and Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google have potentially struggled to keep pace with AI innovations, and many investors see the two companies as the main providers through which billions of people will access AI tools and chatbots.Developing a device is the only way OpenAI and other AI companies could be able to interact with consumers directly, the report noted.

Altman said he and Ive came to believe that current devices would not work. While ChatGPT changed people’s expectations about the power of technology, it is still being used in an old ways — holding a laptop, opening a website, and typing something in and waiting, the report noted. 

"It is not the sci-fi dream of what AI could do to enable you in all the ways that I think the models are capable of,” said Altman.

data.title: BYD sells more EVs than Tesla in Europe for the first time
data.description:
BYD Company (OTCPK:BYDDF) (OTCPK:BYDDY) sold more electric vehicles in Europe than Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) for the first time in its history, according to data from Jato Dynamics. The Chinese automaker registered 7,231 vehicles in during April, which was up 169% year-over-year and slightly higher than Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) tally for the same month. 

Tesla's (TSLA) registrations were estimated to have fallen 49% year-over-year in April amid the Model Y changeover and persistent backlash against Elon Musk for interfering with European politics. The Austin-based company has seen sales in Europe fall dramatically in 2025, despite an overall rise in the EV market.

"Although the difference between the two car brands’ monthly sales totals may be small, the implications are enormous," stated Jato Dynamics analyst Felipe Munoz. "This is a watershed moment for Europe's car market," he added.

Volkswagen (OTCPK:VLKAF) led Europe in battery-electric registrations in April, followed by BMW (BMWYY), Skoda, Audi, Renault SA (OTCPK:RNSDF) (OTCPK:RNLSY), Kia (OTCPK:HYMTF), Mercedes-Benz (OTCPK:MBGAF), Volvo (OTCPK:VLVOF), and Hyundai (OTCPK:HYMTF), per Jato Dynamics.

Earlier in the week, BYD Company (OTCPK:BYDDF) introduced the Dolphin Surf to the European market. The introduction of the new low-cost EV is seen as a significant development in the sector. The Dolphin Surf, BYD's (OTCPK:BYDDF) tenth model for the European market, is priced aggressively, with promotional offers starting at €19,990 and standard pricing beginning at €22,950 for a version with a 322-kilometer range, rising to €24,990 for a model capable of 507 kilometers.

data.title: Thermal Energy to make normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Thermal Energy International (OTCQB:TMGEF) plans to make a normal course issuer bid to purchase for cancellation up to 8,655,715 common shares, representing around 5% of the company’s outstanding common shares.
  • The period during which purchases may be made under the bid will begin on May 22, 2025, and will extend until May 21, 2026, or earlier if the maximum number of shares sought in the bid has been obtained.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: European markets in red as investors scan flurry of economic data, Eurozone output fall
data.description:
Update: The revised post updates market movements as of 7.00am ET.

London (UKX)  -0.72% UK's flash PMI estimates show weaker manufacturing, services recover

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.89%

The HCOB Flash Composite PMI for Germany fell to 48.6 in May 2025 from 50.1 in April, well below forecasts of 50.4. 

France (CAC:IND)  -1.00%

France’s composite PMI hits a 2-month high in May; manufacturing climbs to 28-month peak.

Euro Area flash PMI shows drop in services, new orders fall.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) fell 0.88% on Thursday, with almost every sector in the red, as investors parsed through a flurry of economic data from the region.

The HCOB Eurozone Composite PMI fell to 49.5 in May from 50.4 in the previous month, contrasting sharply from market expectations of 50.7.

On the corporate front, Easyjet (OTCQX:EJTTF) reported a loss for the first six months of the year, but said current bookings indicate it will meet expectations for full-year profit.

Deutsche Bank analysts downgraded British telecom group BT Group (OTCPK:BTGOF).

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 1 basis point to 4.61%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 2.65%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 4.78%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Rio Tinto plans to boost bauxite production at Amrun as other mines close
data.description:
Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) said Thursday it will conduct final engineering studies and early works to increase production capacity at the Amrun bauxite mine in Australia's Queensland state, in a project aimed at ensuring the company has enough bauxite supply for its Australian alumina refineries and customers.

The Kangwinan project would include building a new mine and expanding the existing port to nearly double bauxite production from Rio's (NYSE:RIO) Weipa Southern operations, where Amrun is located.

Production from the Kangwinan project would replace output from the Andoom mine in Queensland and the Gove mine in Australia's Northern Territory, which are both expected to close toward the end of the decade, the company said.

The proposed expansion would increase annual bauxite production capacity from Rio's (RIO) Weipa Southern operations by as much as 20M metric tons from the current capacity of 23M tons, and expand export capacity through the Amrun port.

Rio (RIO) said it intends to make a final investment decision on the project in 2026, with first production targeted for 2029.

data.title: Sharps Technology regains compliance with Nasdaq's listing rules
data.description:
• Sharps Technology (NASDAQ:STSS) has regained compliance with The Nasdaq's Capital Market's minimum bid price requirement of $1.00 per share and the minimum stockholders’ equity requirement of $2.5 million.
  • The company's common stock will continue to be listed and traded on Nasdaq.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Happy Bitcoin Pizza Day! Milestones of the world's top crypto
data.description:
The selloff in bonds took the cheer out of Wall Street on Wednesday, but risk-on sentiment was more than alive elsewhere. Bitcoin (BTC-USD) hit fresh record highs just before "Pizza Day," with the world's most popular crypto climbing to $110,000 and then some. Supportive regulatory developments in the U.S. have added to the momentum as congressional lawmakers near legislation that would establish a regulatory framework for the stablecoin market.

Snapshot: The crypto world didn't get here in a vacuum. In fact, May 22 is heralded as "Bitcoin Pizza Day," in reference to the first commercial Bitcoin (BTC-USD) transaction that took place 15 years ago. On that day in 2010, early adopter and miner Laszlo Hanyecz bought two large Papa John's (PZZA) pizzas for 10,000 BTC, signaling the potential for real-world purchases and Bitcoin as a medium of exchange.

At the time, BTC was only a small development among tech nerds, but would eventually capture the love of investors and the market. Commodity or currency? Regulated or decentralized? Safe haven or risk asset? There have been many questions about Bitcoin along the way, but check out the list of milestones that paved the way toward wider adoption.

 

SA commentary: "Following its recent consolidation phase, Bitcoin is poised for a significant rally, potentially reaching $176,000," writes Investing Group Leader Joe Albano. "The creation of Bitcoin ETFs has significantly boosted retail investor participation, legitimizing the asset class and driving prices higher," adds SA Analyst Dividend Seeker. "Governments, including the US, are now considering Bitcoin strategic reserves, adding a new layer of legitimacy and potential price support."
